intro: Deyeuxia sesquiflora, also known as Sesquiflorous grass or Sesquiflorous Deyeuxia, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia sesquiflora include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and dried powders, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections in traditional Chinese medicine.
